questions.question: Define Exponential Distribution and state its probability density function.
keyPoints: Exponential distribution models time between independent events occurring at constant rate λ
PDF: f(x; λ) = λe^(-λx) for x ≥ 0

questions.question: State and prove the memoryless property of Exponential Distribution.
keyPoints: Memoryless: P(X > s+t | X > s) = P(X > t) for s,t ≥ 0
Proof using survival function: P(X > s+t | X > s) = e^(-λ(s+t))/e^(-λs) = e^(-λt) = P(X > t)
Interpretation: Past waiting time does not affect future waiting probability
Only exponential and geometric distributions have this property

questions.question: Derive the mean and variance of Exponential Distribution.
Show that the sum of n independent Exponential(λ) random variables follows a Gamma distribution.
keyPoints: If X₁, X₂, ..., Xₙ ~ Exp(λ) independent, then Y = ΣXi ~ Gamma(n, λ)
Gamma distribution has shape parameter n and rate parameter λ
Special case: n=1 gives exponential, n=2 gives Erlang distribution

questions.question: Explain the relationship between Poisson Process and Exponential Distribution.
keyPoints: Poisson process: events occur randomly at constant average rate λ
Inter-arrival times (time between consecutive events) follow Exp(λ)
If N(t) ~ Poisson(λt), then Tᵢ (inter-arrival times) are i.i.d. Exp(λ)
This connects discrete counting process to continuous time modeling

questions.question: Derive the Maximum Likelihood Estimator for the parameter λ of Exponential Distribution.
keyPoints: Likelihood function: L(λ) = Πᵢ₌₁ⁿ λe^(-λxi) = λⁿe^(-λΣxi)

questions.question: A telephone line receives calls at an average rate of 3 calls per minute. Assuming exponential distribution, find: (a) Probability of waiting more than 30 seconds (b) Probability of waiting between 10 and 20 seconds.

(a) Define Exponential Distribution and derive its CDF. (b) Prove that Exponential Distribution has constant hazard rate. (c) If component lifetime X ~ Exp(λ=0.001), find probability it survives 1000 hours and its median lifetime.
keyPoints: (a) PDF: f(x)=λe^(-λx), CDF: F(x)=1-e^(-λx) for x≥0

questions.question: (a) State and explain the memoryless property of Exponential Distribution. (b) Prove that if X ~ Exp(λ), then Y = λX ~ Exp(1). (c) A system has two independent components with lifetimes X₁ ~ Exp(0.01) and X₂ ~ Exp(0.02). Find probability the system fails within 50 hours (system fails when any component fails).
keyPoints: (a) Memoryless: P(X>s+t|X>s)=P(X>t), proof using conditional probability

questions.question: (a) Derive mean, variance, and standard deviation of Exponential Distribution. (b) Show that if X ~ Exp(λ), then 2λX ~ χ²(2). (c) Ten observations (in hours): 120, 85, 200, 150, 90, 175, 110, 95, 130, 145. Estimate λ using MLE and find expected lifetime.
keyPoints: (b) Using transformation: P(2λX≤x)=P(X≤x/2λ)=1-e^(-x/2), which is χ²(2) CDF
